title: [circt-verilog] Need to carry macro defined in previous file?
body:
```bash
$ head *.sv
==> A.sv <==
`define FILE_A

==> B.sv <==
`ifndef FILE_A
  `err
`endif

module Top();
endmodule
```
```bash
$ verilator A.sv B.sv --lint-only
- V e r i l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.047 devel rev v5.046-29-ga3870c5e5
- Verilator: Built from 0.045 MB sources in 2 modules, into 0.002 MB in 3 C++ files needing 0.000 MB
- Verilator: Walltime 0.034 s (elab=0.014, cvt=0.015, bld=0.000); cpu 0.005 s on 1 threads; allocated 25.441 MB
$ verilator  B.sv A.sv --lint-only
%Error: B.sv:2:3: Define or directive not defined: '`err'
                : ... Suggested alternative: '`error'
    2 |   `err
      |   ^~~~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.047 for more assistance.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
$ circt-verilog A.sv B.sv
B.sv:2:3: error: unknown macro or compiler directive '`err'
  `err
  ^
```
I tested Yosys and other commercial tools and the macro is expanded in B.sv if A.sv read first. 
Not sure this behavior is well-defined in the SV spec
code_blocks:
